title: histiopteris conspicua
intro: Histiopteris conspicua, also known as the native fern or maori fern, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Dicksoniaceae. It has been used for centuries in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe and protect the skin. The health benefits of Histiopteris conspicua include the ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Historically, the plant has been used to make herbal preparations, including teas, poultices, and infusions, which have been used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall health and well-being.
